functional_requirements.requirement: The system checks if everything is working when powered on.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: The system starts when the power is turned on and checks if everything is working.

functional_requirements.requirement: If there is a problem during startup, the system stops.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If there is a problem, it stops.

functional_requirements.requirement: If everything is fine during startup, the system becomes ready to use.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If everything is fine, it becomes ready to use.

functional_requirements.requirement: When the system is ready to use, the user can reset it.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: When it is ready to use, the user can reset it, activate it, or turn it off.

functional_requirements.requirement: When the system is ready to use, the user can activate it.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: When it is ready to use, the user can reset it, activate it, or turn it off.

functional_requirements.requirement: When the system is ready to use, the user can turn it off.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: When it is ready to use, the user can reset it, activate it, or turn it off.

functional_requirements.requirement: If the system is activated, it starts monitoring.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If activated, the system starts monitoring.

functional_requirements.requirement: If a sensor is triggered while monitoring, the alarm starts and alerts are sent.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If a sensor is triggered, the alarm starts and alerts are sent.

functional_requirements.requirement: If it turns out to be a false alarm or the time ends, the system continues monitoring.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If it turns out to be a false alarm or the time ends, it continues monitoring.

functional_requirements.requirement: At any time, the user can enter the password to stop the system and bring it back to normal.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: At any time, the user can enter the password to stop it and bring it back to normal.

prompt:
You are a requirements engineer.
Extract functional software requirements from the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "functional_requirements": [
    {
      "requirement": "string",
      "evidence_quote": "short exact quote from source text"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
1. Include only system capabilities/behaviors.
2. Exclude background context, impact statements, problem motivation, and pure business goals.
3. Keep each requirement atomic and clear.
4. Preserve original meaning. Do not invent functionality.
5. If uncertain, keep the item but keep evidence_quote explicit.

Source text:
<<<
The system starts when the power is turned on and checks if everything is working. If there is a problem, it stops. If everything is fine, it becomes ready to use.

When it is ready to use, the user can reset it, activate it, or turn it off.

If activated, the system starts monitoring. If a sensor is triggered, the alarm starts and alerts are sent.

If it turns out to be a false alarm or the time ends, it continues monitoring.

At any time, the user can enter the password to stop it and bring it back to normal.
>>>
